title: oxalis convexula
intro: Oxalis convexula, also known as the Mexican wood sorrel or yellow wood sorrel, is a plant species belonging to the oxalis genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Oxalis convexula is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make a poultice or salve for skin conditions and wounds.
